skill.name: nuxt
skill.description: Guides Nuxt 4 development with current patterns for routing, server APIs, middleware, composables, components, and configuration.
skill.summary: Build and maintain Nuxt 4 applications with current framework patterns.
skill.author: onmax

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, and filesystem patterns. Review found these are Markdown reference examples, Nuxt syntax, runtime configuration examples, and official documentation links, not executable skill code. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner treated Markdown backticks, code fences, and Nuxt syntax examples as shell execution. These lines are documentation text and package script examples, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown inline code, tables, or fenced examples. No script file or instruction executes these strings.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The env access hits show standard Nuxt runtime configuration and CI examples. They explain private versus public config and do not send secrets to any endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is framework configuration guidance. The examples keep secrets server-side and use test values for CI.

low_findings.title: Network Patterns Are Examples And Official Links
low_findings.description: Network detections are official documentation URLs and Nuxt data fetching examples. They do not collect local data or perform hidden requests from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to Nuxt, h3, Nitro, GitHub API examples, or local app endpoints. No exfiltration path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Hits Are Token Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak crypto and reconnaissance lines are Nuxt version text, route parameter syntax, and file naming guidance. No cryptographic API or system reconnaissance command appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context contains route syntax like [id] and Nuxt documentation prose. There is no hash function, crypto library, uname, whoami, or host inspection command.

low_findings.title: Hidden File References Are Project Setup Examples
low_findings.description: Hidden file detections refer to expected project paths such as workflow, Nuxt build, and ESLint cache paths. They are not reads of sensitive hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited paths are common build and lint configuration paths. No filesystem read, write, or secret harvesting instruction is present.

content.user_title: Build Nuxt 4 Apps With Current Patterns
content.value_statement: Nuxt projects change quickly, and older patterns can create routing or server mistakes. This skill gives focused Nuxt 4 guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides Nuxt 4 server routes, server middleware, and h3 helpers.
Explains file-based routing, typed navigation, route groups, and layouts.
Covers Nuxt composables such as useFetch, useAsyncData, and useRuntimeConfig.
Recommends NuxtLink, NuxtImg, and NuxtTime for framework-native templates.
Provides configuration patterns for runtime config, modules, auto-imports, and layers.
Includes setup guidance for CI, ESLint, pnpm scripts, and NuxtHub deployment.
limitations: It is reference guidance only and does not install packages or run commands.
It targets Nuxt 4.3 and later, so older Nuxt projects may need changes.
It does not replace official module documentation for new or breaking features.
Generated application code still needs normal security and project review.

use_cases.title: Create Modern Nuxt Routes
use_cases.description: Build pages and nested routes using Nuxt 4 file conventions, typed navigation, and route groups.

use_cases.title: Design Server APIs
use_cases.description: Create server endpoints with clear parameter names, validation, typed errors, cookies, and h3 helpers.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project Setup
use_cases.description: Align configuration, runtime variables, CI steps, linting, package scripts, and deployment patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Add A Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Nuxt skill to add a typed page route for a user profile. Explain the file name and navigation pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Create An API Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Nuxt skill to design a server API route with body validation, clear errors, and a descriptive route parameter.

prompt_templates.title: Review Runtime Config
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Nuxt skill to review my runtime config plan. Separate private secrets from public client values and note risks.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor A Nuxt Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Nuxt skill to refactor an older Nuxt feature to Nuxt 4 patterns across routes, composables, middleware, and server APIs.

output_examples.input: I need a users area with list and detail pages.
output: Recommends a parent users page with nested index and user detail pages.
Uses descriptive route parameters and typed navigation instead of string concatenation.
Flags the need to check generated route names before linking.

output_examples.input: I want a server endpoint that creates a record.
output: Suggests a method-specific server route with body validation and typed errors.
Keeps the route thin by moving data access into server utilities.
Returns the correct created status and avoids leaking internal errors.

output_examples.input: I am configuring API base URLs and secrets.
output: Separates private runtime values from public client values.
Notes that public config must never contain secrets.

best_practices: Load only the reference file that matches the current Nuxt task.
Use typed routes, descriptive parameters, and Nuxt components whenever possible.
Keep secrets in private runtime config and validate required values early.
anti_patterns: Do not apply old Nuxt 2 or Nuxt 3 patterns without checking Nuxt 4 behavior.
Do not use generic route parameters when a domain-specific name is available.
Do not expose runtime secrets through public config or client-side code.

faq.question: Does this skill support Nuxt 4?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on Nuxt 4.3 and later patterns.

faq.question: Can it create server routes?
faq.answer: It can guide server route design, validation, errors, cookies, WebSocket, and server-sent event patterns.

faq.question: Does it run project commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides examples and guidance, but it does not execute commands by itself.

faq.question: Does it cover Nuxt modules?
faq.answer: It covers common configuration patterns. New module details should be checked against official documentation.

faq.question: Can it help migrate older Nuxt code?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights Nuxt 4 routing, server, composable, and component patterns that differ from older code.

faq.question: Is it safe for secrets?
faq.answer: The guidance separates private and public runtime config. Review generated app code before using real secrets.
